mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in top PAYEMS quintiles is not significantly higher than bottom quintiles (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.5 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over two months signals deteriorating household income and corporate earnings prospects, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 2 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and increase in the federal funds rate generates a toxic policy-mismatch signal where tightening monetary policy hits a weakening real economy, producing strongly negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of INDPRO decline and FEDFUNDS increase fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic in a bivariate regression is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, month-over-month growth in retail sales captures transfer-payment-fueled consumption resilience that exceeds depressed expectations, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6.0% and RSAFS grows month-over-month, positive S&P 500 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations, or the mean excess return over cash is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month change in industrial production is a high-frequency proxy for corporate revenue surprise; positive changes drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes fail to outperform negative changes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing starts above 1500 thousand units signal durable construction multiplier effects and cyclical earnings strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with a static near-zero federal funds rate creates stagflationary discount-rate fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 1.5 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth signals peak employment and impending wage-driven margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0% and PAYEMS declines over a 3-month window, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
